steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the linear scale factor from surface area ratio
steps.content:
For similar solids, areas scale by the square of the linear scale factor. Calculate the surface area ratio:
$$\frac{\text{Surface Area of X}}{\text{Surface Area of Y}} = \frac{62}{1,550} = \frac{1}{25}$$
Since $k^2 = \frac{1}{25}$, the linear scale factor is $k = \frac{1}{5}$.

steps.title: Apply volume scaling and sum
steps.content:
Volumes of similar solids scale by the cube of the linear scale factor. With $k = \frac{1}{5}$:
$$\text{Volume of X} = k^3 \times \text{Volume of Y} = \left(\frac{1}{5}\right)^3 \times 1,500 = \frac{1}{125} \times 1,500 = 12 \text{ cm}^3$$
The sum of the volumes is:
$$12 + 1,500 = 1,512 \text{ cm}^3$$
